name: Lemon Meringue Angel Food Cake (Tom Roach's Angel Food Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Place fresh, cold egg whites and cold water in the bowl of a heavy-duty mixer. Set aside until they reach 60 degrees or slightly below room temperature.
2. Pour flour, 1/2 cup granulated sugar, and salt into a triple sifter or sieve. Sift onto a sheet of waxed paper; set aside.
3. Adjust rack to lower third of oven and preheat to 325 degrees. Set nearby a dry, ungreased 10-inch tube pan for baking the cake.
4. Whip egg whites with a whisk attachment until frothy. Add cream of tartar and whip on medium speed until soft peaks form. Gradually add 1 cup granulated sugar while whipping, until the mixture thickens and forms soft droopy white peaks. Add vanilla and lemon zest in the final moments of whipping.
5. Sprinkle one quarter of the flour mixture over the whites, folding gently with a rubber spatula. Repeat with the remaining flour mixture, adding only one quarter at a time.
6. Gently pour the batter into an ungreased tube pan. Bake for 45 to 50 minutes or until the top is light golden, the cake feels spongy and springs back when touched, and a wooden toothpick inserted in the middle comes out clean.
7. Invert the cake onto the neck of a bottle or funnel. Cool completely before removing from the pan.
8. Remove the cake from the pan by sliding the tip of a small metal spatula between the cake and pan. Slowly trace the perimeter to release any cake sticking to the pan, tapping it against the counter as needed.
9. Rotate the cake pan, tapping gently until free. Cover with a rack or cardboard round and invert onto the work surface, tapping firmly to remove the cake from the pan.
10. Lemon Curd Filling:
a. In a 1-1/2 quart heavy-bottomed saucepan, whisk eggs, yolks, sugar, lemon juice, and zest together briefly.
b. Add butter pieces and place over medium heat, stirring constantly until the bottom of the pan begins to brown.
c. Cook without boiling for several minutes, then remove from heat and pour through a stainless steel sieve into a bowl.
d. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate until thickened (about 2 hours).
11. Italian Meringue Frosting:
a. Combine water and 2/3 cup sugar in a saucepan; bring to a boil, dissolving the sugar.
b. Increase heat to medium-high and continue boiling without stirring until a candy thermometer reads 235 degrees.
c. Whisk egg whites with cream of tartar until soft peaks form, then add 2 tablespoons sugar.
d. Continue whipping until stiff but not dry, about 3 minutes.
e. Pour syrup onto the whipped whites while mixer runs; whip on medium speed for an additional 3 minutes or until glossy and stiff peaks form.
f. Add vanilla and cool to room temperature (about 8-10 minutes).
12. Assemble: Cut cake into three layers, spread lemon curd between two layers, and frost top and sides with Italian Meringue Frosting.
